name: Serra d'Oltà and Morro de Toix Loop
name_original: Morro de Toix y vuelta al Dit d'Oltá — Circular desde Calpe por trialeras
description: This difficult 18.6-mile mountain bike route from Calpe tackles technical singletrack and offers panoramic coastal views from Morro de Toix.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Calp offers a diverse landscape, transitioning from coastal areas to rugged mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by challenging limestone formations, pine forests, and significant elevation gains, providing a varied experience for mountain bikers. Iconic landmarks like the Peñón de Ifach serve as dramatic backdrops, while areas such as the Sierra de Oltà feature technical sections with loose rock. This varied topography makes Calp a notable destination for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Calp The most popular mountain bike trail is Morro de Toix y vuelta al Dit d'Oltá — Circular desde Calpe por trialeras, a 18.6 miles (30.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete, featuring challenging singletrack sections and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Casa Coco – Abandoned House Ruins loop from La Merced, a moderate 25.6 miles (41.2 km) path, traversing diverse terrain through pine forests and coastal areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Mirador de la Lloma Plana – Views of the Serra de Bèrnia loop from Cometa III, a 33.5 miles (54.0 km) trail leading through rugged landscapes with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Calp is defined by coastal views, rugged mountainous terrain, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate routes to demanding technical trails. The routes in Calp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2,900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Calp?
A: Calp offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 80 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Calp?
A: Thanks to its agreeable microclimate, Calp is suitable for mountain biking year-round. This makes it a popular destination for cyclists seeking consistent conditions, even during winter months.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Calp?
A: Mountain biking around Calp features a diverse landscape, transitioning from stunning coastal areas to rugged mountainous terrain. You'll encounter challenging limestone formations, dry and technical sections, pine forests, and routes with significant elevation gains. Areas like the Sierra de Oltà are known for their technical sections with loose rock.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Calp?
A: Yes, Calp offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 8 easy mountain bike trails, providing a gentler introduction to the region's diverse terrain. These routes are perfect for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Calp boasts 41 difficult mountain bike trails. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections, particularly in areas like the Sierra de Oltà, which is known for its demanding limestone mass and trails like 'Los Tubos' descent. An example of a challenging route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926093478/morro-de-toix-y-vuelta-al-dit-d-olta-circular-desde-calpe-por-trialeras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morro de Toix y vuelta al Dit d'Oltá — Circular desde Calpe por trialeras&lt;/a&gt;, which includes challenging singletrack sections.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Calp?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Calp are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. This is convenient for planning your ride. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31438953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Coco – Abandoned House Ruins loop from La Merced&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses diverse terrain through pine forests and coastal areas.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Calp?
A: While mountain biking in Calp, you can enjoy stunning coastal views, pine forests, and dramatic mountainous backdrops. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/174441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Ifach summit&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark, and routes often pass through areas like the Sierra de Oltà. You might also encounter notable mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Rates&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1851315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bèrnia Pass&lt;/a&gt;, known for their panoramic views.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes with good viewpoints?
A: Yes, Calp's mountainous and coastal terrain offers numerous routes with excellent viewpoints. Many trails provide stunning vistas of the Mediterranean Sea, the Peñón de Ifach, and the surrounding mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31542665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Lloma Plana – Views of the Serra de Bèrnia loop from Cometa III&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading through rugged landscapes with breathtaking views of the Serra de Bèrnia.

Q: Is Calp suitable for family-friendly mountain biking?
A: Calp offers some options that can be suitable for families, particularly on its easier trails. With 8 easy routes available, families can find less demanding paths to enjoy together. It's advisable to check individual route details for specific suitability and terrain before heading out.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Calp?
A: The mountain biking experience in Calp is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscape, which combines coastal beauty with challenging mountainous terrain, and the variety of trails suitable for different skill levels. The stunning views and the opportunity for year-round cycling are also often highlighted.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that offer views of the Peñón de Ifach?
A: Yes, many routes in and around Calp offer spectacular views of the iconic Peñón de Ifach. This dramatic limestone rock serves as a constant backdrop for rides. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40385944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Coco – View of Peñón de Ifach loop from La Merced&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate path specifically designed to showcase this landmark.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on mountain bike trails in Calp?
A: Mountain bike trails in Calp often feature significant elevation gains due to the region's mountainous terrain. Many routes can involve over 1,000 meters of ascent, providing a substantial physical challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17307239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí de Lliber – Abandoned House Ruins loop from Calp&lt;/a&gt; includes over 1,000 meters of elevation gain.
tips: Rising to around 100 metres above sea level, a walk up the Mirador Monte Toix will give you breathtaking views. It’s an easy-to-moderate hike, just make sure you take your camera, because there are some spectacular scenes when you get to the top. Apparently you can even see Ibiza on a really clear day.
Top location! Beautiful view. Take a short stop and capture the wonderful perspectives.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the "Morro de Toix y vuelta al Dit d'Oltá — Circular desde Calpe por trialeras" route is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride. It requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills due to its significant elevation gain (over 1,000 meters) and technical sections, including "trialeras" with steep, rocky, and narrow paths. Some parts may even require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail features diverse and challenging terrain. You'll encounter a significant amount of singletrack, traversing rugged mountainous areas, challenging limestone formations, and pine forests. The route includes sections of path, street, narrow trail, and road, with the "trialeras" indicating technical, narrow sections often with steps and rocks.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints or landmarks along the trail?
faqs.answer: This route offers breathtaking panoramic and coastal views. Key highlights include the View from Mirador Monte Toix, where you can see much of the northern Costa Blanca, including the Oltá mountain, the Calpe cliff, and even the Ibiza islands on clear days. You'll also get a View of Calpe and Peñón de Ifach, along with vistas of the Serra Gelada, Altea, and Puig Campana mountain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point for parking isn't specified, as a circular route originating from Calpe, you would typically look for public parking within Calpe that allows for easy access to the trail's initial segments. Given the trail's popularity, arriving early is advisable, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Costa Blanca region generally offers good conditions for mountain biking outside of the hottest summer months. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-November) are often ideal, providing milder temperatures and pleasant weather. Winter can also be suitable, but always check local weather conditions before heading out, as some sections might be more challenging when wet.

faqs.question: Is the Morro de Toix y vuelta al Dit d'Oltá trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail itself does not explicitly prohibit dogs. However, given the route's difficult classification, technical terrain, and significant duration (around 3 hours 17 minutes of moving time), it may not be suitable for all dogs. If you bring your dog, ensure they are fit enough for such a demanding ride, are kept on a leash, and that you carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Serra d'Oltà park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the natural areas around Morro de Toix or the Serra d'Oltà for mountain biking. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the environment.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, allowing you to experience diverse scenery without retracing your steps. The full loop is approximately 30.0 km (18.6 miles) long.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other official trails. You will find parts of the PR-CV 340 and the Camí Nou de la Ràpita along the way. A very small portion also touches Partida Estación II.

faqs.question: Given its difficulty, are there any specific strategies for tackling this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Due to the trail's difficult rating and technical "trialeras," it's wise to conserve energy on the climbs and approach descents with caution. Some sections may require dismounting and pushing your bike, so be prepared for that. Starting early in the day can help you avoid the midday heat and allow ample time to complete the 30 km route, which typically takes over 3 hours of active riding.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For this demanding route, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike suitable for technical terrain, a helmet, gloves, and appropriate cycling attire. Essential items include plenty of water (as there's no water available on the trail), energy snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone with the komoot app for navigation.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel near the trailhead in Calpe?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Calpe, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops in the town where you can refuel before or after your ride. Calpe offers a wide range of options to grab a coffee, a meal, or replenish supplies.

waypoints.name: View of Calpe and Peñón de Ifach
